a.xlink:title: Repository: gia Branch: feature/clean-modules Commit: 6761fb2 Update genai to 0.5 and refresh Cargo.lock dependencies Date: 2 days ago
WIP on master: 8d2e7a3 New default audio device detection (#9)

Feature: installation packages for macOS Silicon and macOS Intel (#14)
* Add macOS package builder and installation scripts
Introduced a new directory for macOS package creation, including a `.gitignore`, README, build script (`build-package.sh`), and postinstall script. The installer builds a macOS `.pkg` for the GIA CLI tool, installs binaries to `~/bin`, configures the shell PATH, and removes quarantine attributes.
* Add macOS ARM package build and upload to release workflow
Introduce steps to build and upload a macOS ARM installer package in the GitHub Actions workflow. The `build-package.sh` script now dynamically accepts a version parameter, ensuring version consistency across builds. The release process also includes the macOS ARM package as part of the assets.
* Add macOS package preparation and upload to release workflow
This update introduces steps to build, package, and upload macOS binaries as installers for both Intel and ARM architectures in the manual-release workflow. It ensures the packages are versioned consistently and properly included in the release artifacts. Additionally, the release upload step is updated to handle separate installer files for each architecture.

✨ feat: Audio device selection with CLI and environment variable support (#16)
* feat: Add audio device selection with CLI and env var support
- Add --list-audio-devices to display all available input devices - Add --audio-device <DEVICE> to specify recording device via CLI - Add GIA_AUDIO_DEVICE environment variable for default device - Implement priority system: CLI param > env var > default device - Update audio recording functions to accept device parameter - Add helpful error messages when device not found
* docs: Update README with audio device selection features
* feat(audio): Handle WAV writer completion gracefully 🎙️
The WAV writer is now wrapped in an Option and taken at the end of the recording process. This prevents issues if the writer is accessed after the stream has been dropped and ensures that Arc::try_unwrap is not necessary, which could fail if other threads still held a reference.
* feat: 🚀 Add audio resampling and Opus encoding
Introduces audio resampling capabilities using the rubato crate to ensure compatibility with Opus encoding. The audio is resampled to a target rate of 48000 Hz, and the Opus encoder now supports a wider range of input sample rates and channel counts.
This commit also updates dependencies in Cargo.lock and gia/Cargo.toml to include rubato and its related crates for the new resampling functionality.
* feat(audio): Improve audio device listing and selection
This commit refactors the audio device listing and selection logic for better clarity and efficiency.
Changes:
• Optimized Default Device Handling: The default audio input device name is now fetched once before iterating through all devices. This avoids redundant calls to host.default_input_device(). • Consolidated Default Device Printing: The default device is printed only once, improving the output format. • Simplified Device Skipping: The logic for skipping the default device when iterating through all devices has been made more concise and readable. • Introduced find_device_by_name Helper: A new helper function find_device_by_name is introduced to encapsulate the logic of searching for a device by its name. This promotes code reuse and readability. • Enhanced get_audio_device Function: The get_audio_device function now utilizes the find_device_by_name helper, making the device selection process cleaner. • Improved Error Messages: Error messages related to not finding specified audio devices are more informative.
---------
Co-authored-by: Jochen Schieberlein <jochen.schieberlein@thinprint.com>

feat(audio): Improve audio device listing and selection
This commit refactors the audio device listing and selection logic for better clarity and efficiency.
Changes:
• Optimized Default Device Handling: The default audio input device name is now fetched once before iterating through all devices. This avoids redundant calls to host.default_input_device(). • Consolidated Default Device Printing: The default device is printed only once, improving the output format. • Simplified Device Skipping: The logic for skipping the default device when iterating through all devices has been made more concise and readable. • Introduced find_device_by_name Helper: A new helper function find_device_by_name is introduced to encapsulate the logic of searching for a device by its name. This promotes code reuse and readability. • Enhanced get_audio_device Function: The get_audio_device function now utilizes the find_device_by_name helper, making the device selection process cleaner. • Improved Error Messages: Error messages related to not finding specified audio devices are more informative.

feat: Enhance conversation management and output
This commit introduces several improvements to how conversations are managed and how output is handled.
Key changes include:
• Improved Conversation ID Generation: Conversations now have more descriptive IDs generated from the initial prompt, combining a slug with a short hash, enhancing readability and discoverability. • Flexible Resume Command: The --resume command now accepts conversation indices (from gia -l), hash suffixes, or full IDs, offering more ways to resume specific conversations. • Enhanced Conversation Listing: The gia --list-conversations command now displays conversations in a more user-friendly tabular format including index, preview, ID, and age. • Refined Output Filename Generation: Output files are now named using the conversation ID and a timestamp, ensuring uniqueness and easier association with the conversation. • Code Cleanup and Refactoring: Removed redundant functions and improved the structure of conversation-related logic. • README Update: The README has been updated to reflect the new conversation management and resume command functionalities.

New default audio device detection (#9)
* feat(audio): Add cpal diagnostic for audio device detection
Adds a diagnostic function using cpal to detect and log the default audio input device. This will help in troubleshooting issues where the audio device is not automatically recognized, particularly in environments like Ford vehicles.
* feat: 🎙️ Improve audio device detection and recording
This commit enhances the audio recording functionality by:
Improving default audio device detection:
• The diagnose_cpal_audio_device function now returns an Option<String> representing the detected device, rather than just logging. • When retrieving the default audio device on macOS and Windows, the code now first attempts to use cpal's detected device and matches it with ffmpeg's output for more accurate selection. • Fallback mechanisms are in place for cases where no specific device is found or matched.
Enhancing macOS device selection:
• The ffmpeg command on macOS now supports both numeric indices and device names for selecting the audio input.
Improving user feedback during recording:
• The recording dialog now displays the specific audio device being used, providing clearer information to the user.
Minor logging and output adjustments:
• Refined log messages and eprintln! output for better clarity. • Removed redundant log messages.
* feat: 🎙️ Improve audio device detection and handling
Refactors audio device detection logic to be more robust and consistent.
• Replaces diagnose_cpal_audio_device with get_cpal_default_device for clearer intent. • Updates get_audio_device to use the new CPAL detection function. • Modifies get_default_audio_device to accept and utilize the CPAL detected device for better matching on macOS. • Enhances macOS audio device argument handling in record_audio to use the validated device name. • Removes unnecessary CPAL diagnostics from get_audio_device and get_default_audio_device on Linux. • Adds more comprehensive tests for get_cpal_default_device to ensure functionality and validate device names.
---------
Co-authored-by: Jochen schieberlein <jochen.schieberlein@thinprint.com>

feat(output): 📢 Show audio completion notification only when spinner is inactive
The audio completion notification should not be displayed when the spinner is active, as it can lead to redundant or confusing user feedback. This change ensures the notification is only shown when appropriate, improving the user experience.

e4f3d6a: feat(output): 📢 Show audio completion notification only when spinner is inactive (Jochen Schieberlein, 5 months ago) 319161b: feat(cli): Add spinner option to display visual feedback 🌀 (Jochen Schieberlein, 5 months ago) 9e8cb3c: feat(giagui): Add spinner-only mode and clap argument parsing (Jochen Schieberlein, 5 months ago)

feat(tools): 🔀 Add hybrid search with Gemini grounding support
Implement intelligent search mode selection with user control: - Default: Gemini grounding with Google Search (paid, best quality) - GIA_SEARCH_MODE=duckduckgo: Free DuckDuckGo search - GIA_SEARCH_MODE=brave: Free Brave Search (requires API key)
Key changes: - Replace GIA_SEARCH_API with GIA_SEARCH_MODE for clearer intent - Add google_search_retrieval tool for Gemini models when unset - Automatic fallback to DuckDuckGo for Ollama models - Add logging to inform users about paid vs free options - Update 4 existing tests, add 2 new tests (8 total for search) - Remove unused methods (security_context, is_empty, GiaTool export) - Fix all compiler warnings, format code
Behavior matrix: - Gemini + no GIA_SEARCH_MODE: Google Search grounding (~$0.0005/search) - Gemini + GIA_SEARCH_MODE set: Custom search (free) - Ollama + any setting: DuckDuckGo (free, grounding not supported)
All 108 tests passing, no warnings.
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(gemini): update to gemini-2.5-flash model name
The Gemini 2.0 Flash model has been updated to "Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite". This change affects how the model is referenced in the CLI and GUI. The older "gemini-2.0-flash" and "gemini-2.0-flash-lite" models have been removed from the GUI options. Additionally, the conversation summary now truncates based on character count rather than byte count to ensure accurate representation of multi-byte characters.

feat(mcp): Integrate Model Context Protocol client
Adds the Model Context Protocol (MCP) client functionality to gia, enabling it to connect to external tool servers. This allows gia to extend its capabilities beyond pure LLM interactions by leveraging specialized tools for file operations, git analysis, code analysis, web scraping, and system information.
Key Features:
• TCP Connection: Connects to a unified MCP server on 127.0.0.1:8080. • Tool Discovery: Automatically discovers available tools from the server upon connection, with caching. • Tool Execution: Allows calling specific tools with JSON parameters, including a configurable timeout. • CLI Integration: Introduces the --mcp-server <category> flag to specify which tool category to connect to. • Graceful Fallback: Continues operation with a warning if the MCP server connection fails. • Mock Tools: Implements mock tool implementations for git, code, web, and system categories for initial testing and demonstration. • Error Handling: Provides comprehensive error handling for connection, discovery, and execution failures via the McpError enum. • Logging: Integrates MCP client actions into gia's logging system. • Documentation: Adds detailed documentation to README.md, CLAUDE.md, and new task-specific files (PRD, summary, task list). • Testing: Includes a robust test suite for the MCP client functionality.
Changes:
• Added mcp-tools dependency to gia/Cargo.toml. • Created gia/src/mcp_client.rs for the MCP client implementation. • Added mcp_server: Option<String> to Config and the --mcp-server CLI argument. • Integrated MCP client initialization and tool discovery into gia/src/app.rs. • Updated README.md and CLAUDE.md to reflect MCP integration and usage. • Added new documentation files: tasks/prd-mcp-client-integration.md, tasks/task-list-mcp-client.md, tasks/phase1-mcp-summary.md. • Created integration tests in gia/tests/mcp_client_test.rs.

✨ feat: Consolidate file input handling and documentation
The FILES input source has been unified to simplify the handling of various file types. Instead of separate entries for text and media files, a single FILES entry now encompasses all supported formats, including text, images, audio, video, and PDFs.
The documentation has been updated to reflect this change, clarifying that the -f or --file argument can accept any file extension and that GIA will auto-detect the content type for processing. This change aims to improve user experience by streamlining the input process for file-based operations.
